title: Sodium Alginate Hydrogel with Crosslinking
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bq36h","text":"Instructions for how to make cyanobacteria/algae based sodium alginate based hydrogel","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"rloni","text":"Be careful when pouring boiling hot water, carrying your mug of tea, and ensure it has cooled a bit before you drink it.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"ckuu1","text":"Water, Water kettle, Your favourite mug, PG Tips English teabags, Skimmed (0%) milk, Spoon, McVities Digestive biscuit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Weigh 2.4g sodium alginate, 2.6g methylcellulose, 45 ml of DI water, 0.1M calcium chloride spray (1.1g CaCl2 powder dissolved in 100ml DI water).
Mix sodium alginate, methylcellulose, and DI water in a beaker until most of the large particles are dissolved.

It is a bit tricky to mix all of the material at once. It could be easier to add the dry powders first and DI water portion by portion to mix all material in a gelatinous consistency. Use an overhead mixer if making a large portion of the gel.
Seal the material mix on the side for 30 minutes.
Add 1% of microbial culture in the hydrogel with a spatula. Mix the culture and hydrogel well into a homogeneous culture.
Use the microbial hydrogel in applications like biophotovoltaics or engineered living materials.
Depends on uses, crosslink the alginate material by spraying with a 0.1M CaCl2 mist on the top surface or soaking it in a 0.1M CaCl2 bath for cross surface crosslinking effect.
creator: Miranda Shou
